frame_analyses.description: Female presenter in black v-neck top against office building background with World Laparoscopy Hospital branding
Title overlay 'Do's & Don'ts after Laparoscopic Surgery' with subtitle about following guidelines
Presenter providing post-operative care instructions with subtitle about cleaning incision site

verbal_cues: Gently clean the incision site with mild soap and water and pat it dry
This helps prevent blood clots and promotes healing
Choose loose-fitting clothing that won't put pressure on the incision area
Maintain a healthy diet: Follow a balanced diet rich in fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean proteins
Attend follow-up appointments: Schedule and attend all post-operative appointments with your surgeon
Avoid lifting heavy objects or engaging in strenuous activities
Lifting heavy objects can strain the abdominal muscles and increase the risk of hernia or other complications
Report any abnormal symptoms or concerns to your surgeon promptly
Avoid picking at or scratching the incision site
Don't discontinue prescribed medications without consulting your surgeon
Alcohol and smoking can impair the healing process and increase the risk of complications
The healing time after laparoscopic surgery varies, but typically the initial recovery period is shorter
By 4-6 weeks, incisions are typically well-healed, energy levels improve, and normal activities can be resumed
However, complete healing may take several months depending on the surgery and individual factors
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding heavy lifting to prevent hernia
Not discontinuing medications without medical consultation
Avoiding alcohol and smoking during recovery

confidence_rationale: Clear instructional video about post-operative care after laparoscopic surgery. No FLS task performance visible.
technique_summary: This is an educational video providing post-operative care instructions for laparoscopic surgery patients, not an FLS task performance or demonstration.
